text: I believe people are fundamentally good.
I think life has a deeper meaning beyond what we can see.
I believe we largely make our own luck.
Living in the present matters more to me than planning for the future.
I believe everything happens for a reason.
A meaningful life matters more to me than a happy one.
I think most things in life are within our control.
I try to live by a clear set of principles.
I think it's better to take risks than to play it safe in life.
I believe my purpose is something I have to discover, not something I create.
What do I think gives life the most meaning?
Which comes closest to how I see the world?
What do I believe shapes our lives the most?
options: A mix of all of it

text: When facing a tough choice, what do I lean on most?

Staying informed about current events is important to me.
I feel a responsibility to contribute to my community.
I can stay close to people whose political views differ sharply from mine.

Honesty matters to me even when the truth is uncomfortable.
Small white lies are acceptable if they spare someone's feelings.
I believe right and wrong depend on the situation.
I'd speak up if I saw something unfair, even at a cost to myself.
Loyalty to people I love can outweigh strict rules.
I try hard to live by the same standards I hold others to.

My conscience bothers me even over small wrongs.
Keeping a promise is something I take very seriously.

options: How it feels in my gut

text: Which value do I hold most sacred?

Learning about other cultures excites me.

Holidays and cultural celebrations mean a lot to me.

text: How do I feel about blending two different cultures in a relationship?
options: I love the richness of it
